pairs.question: What does 'DOS 7' refer to in the context of Russian spacecraft?
pairs.answer: In the context of Russian spacecraft, 'DOS 7' refers to the Mir Core Module, also known as DOS-7. It was a key component of the Mir space station, serving as its core module.

pairs.question: Which versions of DR-DOS were released as successors to Novell DOS 7?
pairs.answer: The versions of DR-DOS that succeeded Novell DOS 7 were OpenDOS 7.01 (1997), DR-OpenDOS 7.02 (1997), DR-DOS 7.02 (1998), DR-DOS 7.03 (1999), DR-DOS 7.04 (LBA/FAT32-enabled OEM), DR-DOS 7.05 (LBA/FAT32-enabled OEM), DR-DOS 7.06 (LBA/FAT32-enabled OEM), and DR-DOS 7.07 (LBA/FAT32-enabled OEM).

pairs.question: How does MS-DOS 7.1 differ from MS-DOS 7.0 in terms of features and release context?
pairs.answer: MS-DOS 7.0 was an LBA-enabled DOS component bundled with Windows 95 in 1995, while MS-DOS 7.1 was an LBA/FAT32-enabled version bundled with Windows 98 and Windows 98 SE in 1998 and 1999, respectively. The key difference is the support for FAT32 file system in MS-DOS 7.1.

pairs.question: Provide a code snippet to check if a system is running a version of DOS 7 or later that supports LBA.
What are the differences between PC DOS 7, PC DOS 2000, and PC DOS 7.10?
pairs.answer: PC DOS 7 was released in 1995 as the successor to PC DOS 6.3. PC DOS 2000 succeeded PC DOS 7 in 1998, and PC DOS 7.10 was an LBA/FAT32-enabled OEM version that succeeded PC DOS 2000. The main differences are the release years and the addition of LBA/FAT32 support in PC DOS 7.10.

pairs.question: Which DOS variants are classified as 32-bit DOS?
pairs.answer: The 32-bit DOS variants include PTS-DOS 7.0 (released in 1995) and PTS-DOS 32. These variants were designed to leverage 32-bit processing capabilities.

pairs.question: What is ROM-DOS 7.1, and how does it differ from other DOS 7 variants?
pairs.answer: ROM-DOS 7.1 is an LBA/FAT32-enabled variant of the ROM-DOS family. Unlike other DOS 7 variants that were typically installed on disk, ROM-DOS 7.1 was designed for embedded systems and firmware, where it could be stored in read-only memory (ROM).
